Title: Modeling bacterial growth and Allee effect via Allen-Cahn theoretical framework
Authors: Anna Maslovskaya, Christina Kuttler, Yixuan Shuai, Alexander Chebotarev, Andrey Kovtanyuk
Quote: The Allen-Cahn-based model of bacterial colony evolution combined with models of nutrient concentration changes and bacterial quorum sensing provides a theoretical justification for the modeling and in silico studies of the evolution of bacterial communities cultivated in nutrient medium.

Title: Generalized logistic model of bacterial growth
Authors: Anna Lo Grasso, Ada Fort, Fariba Fahmideh Mahdizadeh, Agnese Magnani, Chiara Mocenni
Quote: The work proposes a new mathematical model describing the dynamics of growing bacterial cultures as a first order non-linear differential equation as a generalization of the logistic equation, compared with the most studied mathematical models.

Title: Mathematical Model for Growth and Rifampicin-Dependent Killing Kinetics of Escherichia coli Cells
Authors: Researchers from Sabanci University and Dokuz Eylul University
Quote: The mathematical relationship between the concentration of rifampicin and the growth and killing kinetics of Escherichia coli cells is proposed using the solution form of a second-order linear differential equation for a damped oscillator applied to time-killing curve data.

Title: Mathematical modelling of microbes: metabolism, gene expression and growth
Authors: Hidde de Jong, Stefano Casagranda, Nils Giordano, Eugenio Cinquemani, Delphine Ropers, Johannes Geiselmann, Jean-Luc Gouzé
Quote: A general framework for the kinetic modelling of microbial growth is derived from basic hypotheses about the underlying reaction systems, revealing how different modelling approaches are related at a deeper level.

Title: New Insights into Modelling Bacterial Growth with Reference to the Fish Pathogen Flavobacterium psychrophilum
Authors: Christopher D. Powell, Secundino López, James France
Quote: Two new models based upon the principles of Baranyi and co-workers using dampening functions to suppress potential growth, represented by a logistic and derived from rate:state differential equations, resulting in two newly derived growth equations: logistic × hyperbola and logistic × exponential.

Title: Mathematical Modeling for Fermentation Systems: A Case Study in Probiotic Beer Production
Quote: Mathematical modeling of fermentation kinetics is a crucial tool to adequately describe and improve the performance of functional beer fermentation using probiotic bacterial and yeast strains.
